extracted_information: Kubernetes Resource Quotas are a tool for cluster administrators to limit aggregate resource consumption per namespace in a multi-tenant cluster. They provide constraints to ensure fair sharing of resources and prevent one team or user from consuming more than their allocated share. Resource Quotas can limit infrastructure resources (CPU, memory), extended resources (e.g., GPU), storage resources (PersistentVolumeClaims, storage requests per StorageClass, ephemeral storage), and the total number of API objects of specific kinds within a namespace.

ResourceQuotaObject.definition: Provides constraints that limit aggregate resource consumption per namespace.
ResourceQuotaObject.limitations: Can limit quantity of objects by API kind and total amount of infrastructure resources.
ResourceQuotaObject.naming_convention: Must be a valid DNS subdomain name.
ResourceQuotaObject.enforcement: Control plane rejects requests with `403 Forbidden` if quota violated. Users must specify requests/limits for CPU/memory if quotas are enabled for these resources.

InfrastructureResources.cpu: Across all pods in a non-terminal state, sum of CPU requests cannot exceed this value.
InfrastructureResources.memory: Across all pods in a non-terminal state, sum of memory requests cannot exceed this value.
InfrastructureResources.limits.cpu: Across all pods in a non-terminal state, sum of CPU limits cannot exceed this value.
InfrastructureResources.limits.memory: Across all pods in a non-terminal state, sum of memory limits cannot exceed this value.
InfrastructureResources.hugepages-: Across all pods in a non-terminal state, number of huge page requests of specified size cannot exceed this value.

ExtendedResources.description: Quota support added in Kubernetes 1.10. Overcommit is not allowed. Only quota items with prefix `requests.` are allowed.
ExtendedResources.example: requests.nvidia.com/gpu: 4 (limits total requested GPUs in a namespace to 4).

StorageResources.requests.storage: Across all persistent volume claims, sum of storage requests cannot exceed this value.
StorageResources.persistentvolumeclaims: Total number of PersistentVolumeClaims that can exist in the namespace.
StorageResources..storageclass.storage.k8s.io/requests.st: Across all persistent volume claims associated with the specified StorageClass, sum of storage requests cannot exceed this value.
StorageResources..storageclass.storage.k8s.io/persistentv: Across all persistent volume claims associated with the specified StorageClass, total number of PersistentVolumeClaims that can exist in the namespace.

LocalEphemeralStorage.requests.ephemeral-storage: Across all pods in the namespace, sum of local ephemeral storage requests cannot exceed this value.
LocalEphemeralStorage.limits.ephemeral-storage: Across all pods in the namespace, sum of local ephemeral storage limits cannot exceed this value.

ObjectCount.generic_syntax: count/. (for non-core API groups), count/ (for core API group).
ObjectCount.purpose: Protects against exhaustion of control plane storage (e.g., too many Secrets, Jobs). Applies to Kubernetes APIs and CustomResourceDefinitions.
ObjectCount.pods_specialized_quota: Counts and enforces maximum on non-terminal pods to avoid exhausting Pod IPs.

QuotaScopes.description: Each quota can have an associated set of `scopes`. A quota measures usage only if it matches the intersection of enumerated scopes. Resources specified outside allowed set result in validation error.

supported_scopes.BestEffort: Match pods that have best effort quality of service.
supported_scopes.NotBestEffort: Match pods that do not have best effort quality of service.
supported_scopes.PriorityClass: Match pods that references the specified priority class.
supported_scopes.CrossNamespacePodAffinity: Match pods that have cross-namespace pod (anti)affinity terms.
supported_scopes.VolumeAttributesClass: Match persistentvolumeclaims that references the specified volume attributes class.

scopeSelector_operators.In: values field must have at least one value.
scopeSelector_operators.NotIn: values field must have at least one value.
scopeSelector_operators.Exists: values field must NOT be specified.
scopeSelector_operators.DoesNotExist: values field must NOT be specified.

ResourceQuotaPerPriorityClass.description: Control pod's resource consumption based on its priority using `scopeSelector`.
ResourceQuotaPerPriorityClass.example_config: YAML example provided for 'high', 'medium', 'low' priority classes with specific CPU/memory/pod limits.

CrossNamespacePodAffinityQuota.purpose: Limits which namespaces are allowed to have pods with affinity terms that cross namespaces (`namespaces` or `namespaceSelector` fields). Prevents anti-affinity blocking scheduling.
CrossNamespacePodAffinityQuota.disallow_config: ResourceQuota object in namespace with `CrossNamespacePodAffinity` scope and `hard: pods: '0'`.
CrossNamespacePodAffinityQuota.limited_resource_config: Use `kube-apiserver --admission-control-config-file` with `ResourceQuotaConfiguration` to make `pods` with `CrossNamespacePodAffinity` a limited resource. Requires a quota object with `CrossNamespacePodAffinity` scope and hard limit >= number of pods using those fields.

ResourceQuotaPerVolumeAttributesClass.purpose: Control PVC storage consumption based on associated volume attributes classes (`spec.volumeAttributesClassName`, `status.currentVolumeAttributesClassName`, `status.modifyVolumeStatus.targetVolumeAttributesClassName`).
ResourceQuotaPerVolumeAttributesClass.example_config: YAML example provided for 'gold', 'silver', 'copper' volume attributes classes with specific storage requests/PVC counts.

features: Limits aggregate resource consumption per namespace.
Can limit compute resources (CPU, memory), extended resources, storage resources, and object counts.
Enforces explicit resource requests/limits for pods if quotas are set for those resources.
Rejects resource creation/updates that violate quota constraints with HTTP 403 Forbidden.
Allows for scoping quotas to apply only to specific types of pods (e.g., by QoS, termination status, priority class) or PVCs (by volume attributes class).
Protects control plane storage by limiting API object counts.
Enables multi-tenancy by isolating resource consumption across teams/namespaces.
Supports detailed tracking of used vs. hard limits via `kubectl describe quota`.

references.name: Quota support for priority class design document
Detailed example for how to use resource quota
